notes: smolagents ToolCallingAgent — OpenAI o3 (reasoning=high). @tool decorator introspects docstrings + type annotations. Eval details: {'correct_category': True, 'has_reasoning': True, 'mentions_fidelity': True}
raw_output:
Classification: financial_statement

Why Reducto (and we) classify it this way
1. Document‐level cues
   • Title block contains “INVESTMENT REPORT” and “Portfolio Summary” – classic headings for brokerage or bank statements.
   • The very first page shows “Your Portfolio Value”, “Change from Last Period”, beginning/ending portfolio values, and the statement period “July 1 – July 31, 2015”.  These are hall-mark elements of a periodic financial statement.
   • Page footers indicate “1 of 28”, “2 of 28”, etc., characteristic of multi-page monthly/quarterly statements delivered to investors.

2. Section & table structure typical of statements
   • Accounts Included in this Report – lists account names, account numbers, and beginning/ending values.
   • Income Summary, Asset Allocation, Top Holdings – detailed break-downs of income, positions, and percentages; these tables do not appear on invoices, contracts, or blank forms.

3. Vocabulary & regulatory language
   • Brokerage disclaimers: “Brokerage services provided by Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC (FBS), Member NYSE, SIPC”.  SIPC disclaimers are mandated on client statements.
   • References to “statement period”, “year-to-date”, “appreciation or depreciation of your holdings”, etc.

4. Absence of competing-class signals
   • No “Invoice #”, “Bill To”, “Due Date”, or remittance stubs → not an invoice.
   • No clauses beginning “WHEREAS”, signature blocks, or effective-date terms → not a contract.
   • No blank lines, check-boxes, or instructions to “Fill in” → not a form.

Reductoʼs confidence
Reducto returned category = “financial_statement”.  Its criteria-confidence list shows HIGH confidence for the “financial statement” criterion based on the above text patterns (tables of balances, statement dates, brokerage disclosures).  Other categories were either scored 0 or, although sometimes marked 1.0 by the beta confidence reporter, were not chosen as the best overall match.

Therefore the document is best classified as a financial_statement.
